title: VLAN 1 Return Path Failure
summary: Student investigates asymmetric reachability or return-path failure involving VLAN 1.
student_goal: Determine whether traffic can leave but return traffic is blocked, misrouted, or on the wrong VLAN path.

required_evidence.label: Source and destination
required_evidence.description: Student must identify the source, destination, and expected path.

required_evidence.label: Forward path evidence
required_evidence.description: Student must document whether traffic leaves the source network.

required_evidence.label: Return path evidence
required_evidence.description: Student must document whether replies return correctly.

opening_questions: What source and destination are you testing?
Does traffic fail in both directions or only on the return path?
Which VLANs and gateways are involved?
mentor_guidance_rules: Do not treat one-way failure as simple host downtime.
Ask for both forward and return path evidence.
Confirm VLAN and gateway assumptions before suggesting changes.
